items.text:
Stack & Stir Café — Student Union, Level 2

Open Monday through Friday, 7:00 AM to 9:00 PM. Pay at the counter or use your campus meal card.

BREAKFAST (served until 11:00 AM)
- Buttermilk pancake stack — $4.50
- Steel-cut oats with seasonal fruit — $3.75
- Egg and avocado wrap — $5.25
- House yogurt parfait — $3.25

LUNCH AND DINNER
- Grilled chicken salad — $7.50
- Roasted vegetable bowl — $6.25
- House grain bowl with hummus — $6.75
- Daily soup with fresh bread — $4.50

STUDY-HOUR SPECIALS (Tuesday and Thursday, 2:00–4:00 PM only)
- Any drip coffee — $1.50
- Pastry of the day — $2.00
- Combo deal: one drink plus one pastry — $3.00

Bottomless tea is $2.00 with refills until close. Show your valid student ID at the register for ten percent off any sandwich. Please note that the café no longer accepts cash after 7:00 PM; meal cards and credit only. Contact Manager Ms. Owens at owens@union.edu with feedback or questions.

questions.stem: According to the menu, when are study-hour specials available?

options.D: On weekends from noon to evening

questions.explanation: The menu states study-hour specials run 'Tuesday and Thursday, 2:00–4:00 PM only.'

questions.stem: What is the price of the combo deal in the study-hour specials?
questions.explanation: The menu lists 'Combo deal: one drink plus one pastry — $3.00.'

questions.stem: What can be inferred about evening customers who want to pay with cash?

options.A: They receive a small late-night discount.
options.C: They are required to leave a tip.
options.D: They can only buy combo specials.

questions.explanation: Since the café 'no longer accepts cash after 7:00 PM,' cash customers must pay before that time.

items.text:
Updated Printing Quota and Kiosk Notice

Effective Monday, June 15, the library printing system will operate under a new quota and station layout. All current student accounts will be reset to the new monthly limit, and several kiosks will be relocated during the transition week.

Key changes:
- Each undergraduate account receives 200 free pages per month.
- Pages beyond the quota are billed at $0.08 per black-and-white page and $0.25 per color page.
- Unused pages no longer roll over to the next month.
- The third-floor kiosk has been moved to the second-floor reference area near the help desk.
- The basement kiosk will be closed permanently after June 30.

Students who arrive before 9:00 AM may use any open kiosk without a wait. Please load files through the new portal at print.collegeport.edu. The IT help desk in Carver Hall, Room 118, can assist with login problems Monday through Friday, 8:00 AM to 5:00 PM. Contact Mr. Howard at extension 3201 for billing questions.

questions.stem: According to the notice, how many free pages does an undergraduate account receive each month?
questions.explanation: The notice states 'Each undergraduate account receives 200 free pages per month.'

questions.stem: According to the notice, where has the third-floor kiosk been relocated?

options.B: To the basement of the library
options.C: To the IT help desk in Carver Hall

questions.explanation: The notice states the third-floor kiosk 'has been moved to the second-floor reference area near the help desk.'

questions.stem: What can be inferred about students who print color documents?

options.A: They receive an automatic monthly discount.
options.B: They pay more per color page.
options.C: They must request approval from Mr. Howard.
options.D: They cannot use the new printing portal.

questions.explanation: The notice charges '$0.08 per black-and-white page and $0.25 per color page,' so color printing costs more per page.
